roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: Reduced to 3 milestone(s) from 5 due to time budget (179.2h) relative to demand (230.5h).

vision_profile.current_state: A final-year B.Sc Math student with no professional experience, aiming for financial independence.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Lack of foundational programming and web development skills, coupled with fear of competition from peers with more resources.
vision_profile.top_motivation: Achieve financial independence to support her family before her younger brother starts college.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: After this milestone, you can write basic Python programs, understand core data structures, and collaborate using version control, laying the groundwork for any software development role.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are needed to cover the breadth of fundamental programming concepts, from language basics to data handling, problem-solving, and essential collaboration tools.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a problem-solver, equipped with the basic tools to bring ideas to life through code.

project.title: Command-Line Utility for Text Analysis
project.description: Build a Python command-line tool that can read a text file, count words, characters, and identify the most frequent words. The tool should be version-controlled with Git.
project.deliverable: A functional Python script, a Git repository with commit history, and a README.md explaining usage.
seeded_errors: The word counter incorrectly handles punctuation attached to words, requiring regex or string cleaning to fix.

modules.title: Python Fundamentals
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are essential to cover the core syntax, control flow, data types, and modularity required for basic Python programming.

skills.title: Python Syntax & Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering Python basics is the first step for any developer, providing a versatile language foundation for scripting, web development, and data tasks.

scenario.title: Fixing a Basic Python Script

skills.title: Control Flow
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding control flow and functions is crucial for writing logical, reusable, and organized code, a core skill for any software project.

scenario.title: Debugging a Loop Logic Error

skills.title: Variables, Data Types, Operators
skills.why_this_skill: Proficiency with Python's data types and collections is fundamental for storing, organizing, and manipulating data effectively in any application.

scenario.title: Optimizing Data Storage for Performance

skills.title: Modules & Packages
skills.why_this_skill: Learning to organize code into modules and packages is vital for building maintainable and scalable applications, a key aspect of professional development.

scenario.title: Resolving Module Import Errors

skills.title: File Handling
skills.why_this_skill: Robust error handling is critical for building stable applications, preventing crashes, and providing a better user experience.

scenario.title: Implementing Graceful Error Recovery

science.desc: A user reports an unexpected crash in your script due to invalid input; identify the cause and implement error handling.

modules.title: Algorithms & Data Handling
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are necessary to build a foundational understanding of how to efficiently store and process data, which is critical for performance.

skills.title: Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding fundamental data structures is key to choosing the right tools for efficient data storage and retrieval in any programming task.

scenario.title: Choosing the Right Data Structure

skills.title: Algorithmic Thinking
skills.why_this_skill: Developing algorithmic thinking helps in breaking down complex problems into manageable steps, a core skill for efficient coding and problem-solving.

scenario.title: Designing an Algorithm for a New Feature

skills.title: Time & Space Complexity
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding complexity helps in writing efficient code, which is vital for performance-critical applications and a common interview topic.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Slow Function

skills.title: Sorting & Searching
skills.why_this_skill: Efficient searching is a common requirement in applications; mastering these algorithms improves data retrieval performance.

scenario.title: Implementing a Fast Search Feature

skills.title: Sorting & Searching
skills.why_this_skill: Sorting is a fundamental operation in computer science, essential for organizing data and often a prerequisite for other efficient algorithms.

scenario.title: Comparing Sorting Algorithm Performance

science.desc: A large dataset needs to be sorted for display; choose the most efficient algorithm and implement it.

modules.title: Problem Solving & Debugging
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for developing a systematic approach to identifying, analyzing, and resolving issues in code, a core developer competency.

skills.title: Problem Decomposition
skills.why_this_skill: Breaking down complex problems into smaller, manageable parts is a foundational skill for effective problem-solving and software design.

scenario.title: Breaking Down a Complex Feature Request

skills.title: Model Debugging & Explainability
skills.why_this_skill: Effective debugging is an indispensable skill for any developer, enabling quick identification and resolution of issues in code.

scenario.title: Debugging a Runtime Error in a Script

skills.title: Test Case Generation
skills.why_this_skill: Creating effective test cases helps validate code correctness and prevent regressions, ensuring software quality.

scenario.title: Writing Test Cases for a Calculator Function

skills.title: Code Tracing
skills.why_this_skill: The ability to mentally trace code execution is fundamental for understanding program flow and pinpointing logic errors without relying solely on tools.

scenario.title: Tracing a Recursive Function's Execution

skills.title: Logical Error Identification
skills.why_this_skill: Distinguishing between syntax and logical errors is crucial for efficient debugging and developing robust, correct software.

scenario.title: Finding a Subtle Logical Bug in a Calculation

science.desc: A newly implemented feature produces incorrect results under specific conditions; identify the logical flaw and propose a fix.

modules.title: Version Control & Collaboration
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are vital for managing code changes, collaborating with teams, and maintaining a robust development workflow, essential for any professional developer.

skills.title: OOP Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Git is the industry standard for version control, essential for tracking changes, collaborating, and managing codebases effectively.

scenario.title: Recovering from an Accidental Commit

skills.title: GitHub Workflow
skills.why_this_skill: GitHub is central to modern software development, enabling collaboration, code hosting, and project management for teams.

scenario.title: Resolving a Merge Conflict on GitHub

skills.title: Branching & Merging
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering branching and merging is fundamental for collaborative development, allowing multiple developers to work on features concurrently without conflicts.

scenario.title: Managing Feature Branches for a New Release

skills.title: Code Review Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Participating in code reviews improves code quality, shares knowledge, and fosters a collaborative development environment.

scenario.title: Reviewing a Peer's Pull Request

skills.title: Collaborative Development
skills.why_this_skill: Working effectively in a team is crucial for delivering complex software projects and integrating seamlessly into a professional environment.

scenario.title: Coordinating a Feature Implementation with a Teammate

science.desc: Describe a time you used Git to collaborate on a project and how you resolved a conflict.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: After this milestone, you can design and implement modular, reusable code using object-oriented principles and common design patterns, improving code quality and maintainability.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are required to cover the depth of object-oriented programming, design patterns, advanced language features, and code quality practices for building robust applications.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into an architect of clean, organized, and efficient code, building systems that are easy to understand and extend.

project.title: Object-Oriented Library Management System
project.description: Develop an object-oriented Python application for managing a small library. It should include classes for Books, Users, and the Library itself, demonstrating SOLID principles and basic design patterns.
project.deliverable: A Python application with well-defined classes, unit tests, and a design document outlining the OOP structure.
seeded_errors: The system allows a user to borrow a book that is already checked out, requiring a fix in the borrowing logic.

modules.title: OOP & SOLID Principles
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are essential to grasp the core concepts of Object-Oriented Programming and the SOLID principles for designing maintainable and scalable software.

skills.title: Object-Oriented Programming Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: OOP is a fundamental paradigm for structuring complex applications, making code modular, reusable, and easier to manage.

scenario.title: Designing Classes for a Simple Game

skills.title: Encapsulation & Abstraction
skills.why_this_skill: These principles hide implementation details and expose only necessary interfaces, leading to more robust and easier-to-maintain code.

scenario.title: Refactoring for Better Encapsulation

skills.title: Inheritance & Polymorphism
skills.why_this_skill: These concepts enable code reuse and flexible system design, allowing for extensible and adaptable software architectures.

scenario.title: Extending Functionality with Inheritance

skills.title: SOLID Principles
skills.why_this_skill: SOLID principles are guidelines for writing maintainable, scalable, and testable object-oriented code, crucial for long-term project health.

scenario.title: Applying SRP to a God Object

skills.title: Interface Segregation Principle (ISP)
skills.why_this_skill: ISP ensures that clients only depend on the interfaces they actually use, leading to more cohesive and decoupled systems.

scenario.title: Splitting a Generic Interface into Specific Ones

Production debugging scenario for milestone skills

modules.title: Design Patterns
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are needed to understand and apply common design patterns, which provide proven solutions to recurring software design problems.

skills.title: State Pattern
skills.why_this_skill: Creational patterns provide flexible ways to create objects, decoupling the client from the concrete implementation, improving system flexibility.

scenario.title: Implementing a Logger with Singleton Pattern

skills.title: Creational & Structural Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Structural patterns simplify the design by identifying simple ways to realize relationships between entities, making systems more flexible and efficient.

scenario.title: Integrating an Old API with an Adapter

skills.title: Behavioral Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Behavioral patterns focus on communication between objects, improving flexibility and reusability by defining how objects interact.

scenario.title: Implementing Undo/Redo with Command Pattern

skills.title: Behavioral Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Knowing how to apply design patterns in real-world scenarios is crucial for writing elegant, maintainable, and scalable code.

scenario.title: Designing a Notification System with Observer Pattern

skills.title: Anti-Patterns Identification
skills.why_this_skill: Recognizing anti-patterns helps avoid common pitfalls in software design, leading to more robust and maintainable systems.

scenario.title: Refactoring a System with Multiple Anti-Patterns

science.desc: A legacy system is hard to maintain due to tightly coupled components; identify the anti-patterns and suggest refactoring strategies.

modules.title: Advanced Python Programming
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are necessary to deepen Python proficiency, enabling more advanced and efficient code writing for complex applications.

skills.title: Decorator
skills.why_this_skill: Decorators allow for clean, reusable code modification, while generators provide memory-efficient iteration, both crucial for advanced Python development.

scenario.title: Implementing a Caching Decorator

skills.title: Context Managers
skills.why_this_skill: Context managers ensure proper resource management (e.g., file handling, database connections), preventing leaks and improving code reliability.

scenario.title: Ensuring Resource Cleanup with Context Managers

skills.title: Functional Programming in Python
skills.why_this_skill: Functional programming concepts lead to more concise, testable, and parallelizable code, improving overall software quality.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Loop with Map/Filter

skills.title: Asynchronous Programming Basics
skills.why_this_skill: Asynchronous programming is crucial for building responsive and high-performance applications, especially in web development and I/O-bound tasks.

scenario.title: Making Multiple API Calls Concurrently

skills.title: Metaclasses & Descriptors
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding metaclasses and descriptors allows for powerful customization of class and object behavior, enabling advanced framework development.

scenario.title: Creating a Custom ORM Field with Descriptors

modules.title: Code Quality & Refactoring
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are critical for writing clean, maintainable, and understandable code, which is essential for long-term project success and team collaboration.

skills.title: Code Smells Detection
skills.why_this_skill: Identifying code smells helps pinpoint areas in the codebase that indicate deeper problems, guiding refactoring efforts for better quality.
lessons: Common Code Smells (Long Method, Duplicated Code)

scenario.title: Identifying Code Smells in a Legacy Function

skills.title: Refactoring Techniques
skills.why_this_skill: Refactoring improves code structure without changing external behavior, making it easier to understand, maintain, and extend.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Long and Complex Function

skills.title: Clean Code Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Adhering to clean code principles makes software easier to read, understand, and modify, reducing bugs and development costs.

scenario.title: Improving Readability of a Complex Algorithm

skills.title: Static Code Analysis
skills.why_this_skill: Static analysis tools automatically detect potential bugs, style violations, and security vulnerabilities, improving code quality early in the development cycle.

scenario.title: Setting Up a Linter for a New Project

skills.title: Code Documentation
skills.why_this_skill: Good documentation makes code understandable for other developers and your future self, reducing onboarding time and maintenance effort.

scenario.title: Documenting a Complex Python Module

science.desc: Explain the SOLID principles and provide an example of how you would apply one in a real-world scenario.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: After this milestone, you can design simple data models, interact with databases using SQL, and build basic APIs to expose data, forming the backend foundation for web applications.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 4 modules are necessary to cover database design, data persistence, API design principles, and basic API implementation, which are core for data-driven applications.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a bridge between data and applications, capable of structuring information and making it accessible through well-defined interfaces.

project.title: Simple Blog API with Database
project.description: Build a basic RESTful API for a blog using Python and a relational database. The API should allow creating, reading, updating, and deleting blog posts and comments.
project.deliverable: A functional API with database integration, API documentation (e.g., OpenAPI spec), and a deployed instance.
seeded_errors: The API allows deleting a blog post without deleting its associated comments, leading to orphaned data in the database.

modules.title: Database Design & Modeling
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are crucial for designing efficient and well-structured databases, which is fundamental for any data-driven application.

skills.title: Relational Database Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding relational databases is foundational for storing and managing structured data, a core component of most applications.

scenario.title: Designing a Schema for an E-commerce Product Catalog

skills.title: ER Diagrams
skills.why_this_skill: ER diagrams are essential for visually representing database schemas, facilitating communication and design clarity among developers.

scenario.title: Creating an ER Diagram for a Social Media App

skills.title: ORM
skills.why_this_skill: Normalization reduces data redundancy and improves data integrity, leading to more efficient and reliable database systems.

scenario.title: Normalizing a Denormalized Customer Table

skills.title: SQL Schema Design
skills.why_this_skill: Designing effective SQL schemas is crucial for building performant and maintainable relational databases.

scenario.title: Designing a Database Schema for a Project Management Tool

skills.title: NoSQL Database Overview
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding NoSQL databases expands your data storage options, allowing you to choose the best tool for different data models and scalability needs.

scenario.title: Deciding Between SQL and NoSQL for a New Feature

modules.title: Data Handling & Persistence
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are essential for interacting with databases, performing CRUD operations, and ensuring data integrity in applications.

skills.title: SQL Queries (CRUD)
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering SQL CRUD operations is fundamental for any developer working with relational databases, enabling data manipulation and retrieval.

scenario.title: Retrieving Complex Data with Joins

skills.title: ORM
skills.why_this_skill: ORMs simplify database interactions by mapping database tables to objects, allowing developers to work with data using their preferred programming language.

scenario.title: Implementing Data Access with an ORM

skills.title: Vector Databases
skills.why_this_skill: Transactions ensure data consistency and integrity by grouping multiple operations into a single, atomic unit, preventing partial updates.

scenario.title: Handling a Failed Money Transfer with Transactions

skills.title: Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Validating input data is crucial for preventing errors, security vulnerabilities, and maintaining data integrity in your applications.

scenario.title: Implementing Robust User Input Validation

skills.title: File I/O & Serialization
skills.why_this_skill: Handling file input/output and data serialization is essential for reading from and writing to files, and for data exchange between systems.

scenario.title: Parsing and Storing Configuration from a JSON File

science.desc: A new feature requires storing user preferences; design a database schema and implement CRUD operations for it.

modules.title: API Design Fundamentals
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills are critical for designing clear, consistent, and effective APIs, which are the backbone of modern web applications.

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: REST is the most common architectural style for web services; understanding its principles is essential for building and consuming web APIs.

scenario.title: Designing RESTful Endpoints for a User Management System

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: Properly defining API endpoints and resources is key to creating intuitive and consistent APIs that are easy to use and understand.

scenario.title: Refining API Endpoints for a Photo Sharing App

skills.title: HTTP Methods & Status Codes
skills.why_this_skill: Correctly using HTTP methods and status codes is essential for building semantically correct and easily debuggable RESTful APIs.

scenario.title: Choosing Correct HTTP Status Codes for API Responses

skills.title: ORM
skills.why_this_skill: JSON is the de-facto standard for data exchange in web APIs; understanding its structure and usage is critical for API development.

scenario.title: Designing a Consistent JSON API Response Structure

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: Versioning APIs allows for backward compatibility and smooth evolution of services, crucial for long-term maintainability and client satisfaction.
